To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$.

Let the matrix $A$ be:
$$ A = \begin{pmatrix} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{pmatrix} $$

The characteristic polynomial $p(\lambda)$ is defined as:
$$ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) = \det \begin{pmatrix} \frac{189}{16} - \lambda & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} - \lambda & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} - \lambda \end{pmatrix} $$

To simplify the calculation, let's factor out $\frac{1}{16}$ from the entire matrix. Note that each entry can be written with a denominator of 16:
$$ A = \frac{1}{16} \begin{pmatrix} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{pmatrix} $$
Let $B = 16A = \begin{pmatrix} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{pmatrix}$.
